consent.heading: Informed Consent Form
body: This test is part of a scientific study on attention and emotional processing.
You will see words printed in different colors. Your task is to identify the COLOR of the word, not its meaning.
Participation is entirely voluntary and you may withdraw at any time without any consequences.
All of your data is stored anonymously and used for research purposes only.
The test takes approximately 10 to 15 minutes.
consent.checkbox_label: I have read the consent form and agree to participate in this study.
consent.must_agree_error: You must agree to the terms to continue.

instructions.heading: Instructions
pages:
On each trial a word will appear on the screen in one of the four colors below.
You must respond only to the COLOR of the word and ignore its meaning.

The response keys on your keyboard are as follows:
Press the key matching the word's color as quickly and accurately as you can.

First you will complete a few practice trials to get familiar with the task, with feedback.
Then the main test will begin.

practice.start_body: You will now complete a few practice trials. Respond to the color of the word.
practice.end_body:
Practice complete. The main test will now begin.
You will not receive feedback in the main test. Respond as quickly and accurately as possible.

classic.start_body:
In this part, a color NAME is shown whose font color may match its meaning (congruent) or differ from it (incongruent).
Again, respond only to the COLOR of the text, not the word's meaning.

results.heading: Your Results Summary
results.intro: The table below shows your performance in the main test. Reaction time is computed from correct responses only.
results.bias_negative_label: Bias to negative words (negative minus neutral)
results.bias_positive_label: Bias to positive words (positive minus neutral)
results.interp_negative: Your reaction time for negative emotional words was noticeably longer than for neutral words. This pattern may indicate greater attentional capture by negative emotional content.
results.interp_positive: Your reaction time for positive words was longer than for neutral words, which may indicate attentional engagement with positive content.
results.interp_both: Your reaction time was longer for both negative and positive words than for neutral words, suggesting emotional content (regardless of valence) captured more of your attention.
results.interp_none: No substantial difference was found between your reaction times across categories, meaning the emotional meaning of the words had little effect on your performance.
results.interp_stroop_effect: Your reaction time was longer for incongruent than congruent words — this is the classic Stroop effect, showing that word meaning was processed automatically and interfered with naming the color.
results.interp_stroop_none: No substantial Stroop effect was found in your data.
results.disclaimer: Note: these results are for educational and research purposes only and are in no way a clinical diagnosis or assessment.

end.heading: End of Test
end.body: Thank you for participating! Your data has been recorded successfully.

common.fullscreen_message: To start the test, we will enter fullscreen mode.
